source_jobs.title: Short-Form Video Growth Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.


JOB TITLE: SHORT-FORM VIDEO GROWTH SPECIALIST



Location: Remote


Time Zone: US Time Zones (EST–PST)




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a Short-Form Video Growth Specialist to drive audience growth and engagement through high-impact video content across social platforms. This role focuses on rapid experimentation, performance analysis, and continuous optimization of short-form video assets to support brand awareness, demand generation, and conversion goals.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Produce and optimize short-form video content for platforms such as TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

 - Develop compelling hooks, pacing, and captions tailored to platform algorithms and user behavior

 - Support growth and demand campaigns with video assets for organic and paid distribution

 - Track video performance and iterate based on engagement, retention, and conversion data

 - Maintain consistent visual identity and messaging across all video outputs

 - Partner with Marketing and Growth teams to align video strategy with funnel objectives
   
   


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Hands-on experience creating and optimizing short-form video content for social media growth

 - Strong understanding of video engagement metrics, retention curves, and optimization strategies

 - Prior remote work experience is mandatory, including fluency with tools such as Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana (or similar), and experience working with US or UK-based companies; applications without this experience will not be considered

 - Ability to test, iterate, and improve content quickly based on performance data

 - Strong time management and attention to detail
   
   


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience supporting growth, demand generation, or paid social campaigns

 - Familiarity with A/B testing creative concepts, hooks, and formats

 - Experience in fast-paced, experimentation-driven content environments
   
   


TOOLS & TECHNOLOGY

 - Video Editing: Adobe Premiere Pro, CapCut, Final Cut Pro

 - Analytics: Native platform insights, Google Analytics

 - Growth & Distribution: Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads, scheduling tools

 - Collaboration: Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, or similar
   
   


PLEASE NOTE

It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps—the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Technical Customer Enablement & Training Manager
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.


JOB TITLE: TECHNICAL CUSTOMER ENABLEMENT & TRAINING MANAGER



Location: Remote
Time Zone: U.S. Time Zones (EST–PST)
Role Type: Full-Time




ROLE OVERVIEW

The Technical Customer Enablement & Training Manager is responsible for ensuring enterprise customers successfully adopt and scale a modern workflow and automation platform. This is a highly customer-facing role that blends technical fluency, marketing operations knowledge, and structured training delivery.

You will guide customers from first login through full adoption by leading onboarding programs, live training sessions, and cohort-based enablement workshops. The ideal candidate comes from a well-known SaaS or tech company, is comfortable working with code and data, and enjoys teaching marketing teams how to operationalize technology to drive business results.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own customer onboarding from initial setup through full platform adoption

 - Deliver live training sessions, onboarding programs, and cohort-based workshops for enterprise clients

 - Translate technical workflows, automations, and data concepts into simple, actionable steps for marketing teams

 - Build and maintain scalable enablement playbooks, guides, and training materials

 - Support marketing operations workflows, including CRM automation, reporting, and campaign setup

 - Partner with marketing and content teams on SEO, content workflows, and enablement initiatives

 - Act as a trusted, technical-facing advisor for customers throughout the lifecycle
   
   


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2–4 years of experience in customer enablement, customer success, marketing operations, or product training within a SaaS or tech company

 - Working experience at a recognized or well-known technology company strongly preferred

 - Hands-on experience with HubSpot or similar CRM / marketing automation platforms

 - Proficiency in SQL and Python, with the ability to actively use both in workflows, reporting, or training contexts

 - Non-negotiable: Prior remote work experience, fluency with remote collaboration tools (Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana or similar), and experience working with U.S. or UK-based companies. Applications without this experience will not be considered.

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to teach technical concepts to non-technical users
   
   


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Background working with marketing teams or within a marketing agency environment

 - Experience supporting SEO, content operations, or campaign automation

 - Exposure to AI-driven tools, workflow automation, or data-driven marketing platforms
   
   


TOOLS & TECHNOLOGY

 - HubSpot or equivalent CRM/marketing automation platform

 - SQL, Python

 - Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace

 - Workflow automation tools (Zapier or similar)

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Technical Solutions Builder – Marketing AI
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.


JOB TITLE

Technical Solutions Builder – Marketing AI

Location: Remote
Time Zone: US Time Zones (EST–PST)




ROLE OVERVIEW

The Technical Solutions Builder – Marketing AI works directly with customers to design and build technical solutions within our platform. This role combines solutions engineering, automation development, and client partnership. You will analyze marketing workflows, develop custom automations, and ensure successful implementation of AI-driven solutions that improve marketing performance and operational efficiency.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Conduct discovery sessions to understand client marketing and SEO workflows and identify automation opportunities

 - Build custom automations, integrations, and data pipelines using Python and SQL

 - Own complex technical support escalations from issue triage through solution delivery

 - Collaborate with clients to refine workflows and drive adoption of AI-powered processes

 - Contribute to internal tools, reusable automation templates, and implementation documentation

 - Partner with Product, Engineering, and Customer Success teams to improve customer outcomes




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong proficiency in Python and SQL with the ability to build automation workflows and integrations

 - Experience in technical customer-facing roles such as Implementation Specialist, Solutions Consultant, or Marketing Technologist

 - Knowledge of digital marketing workflows including SEO, content operations, or marketing automation

 - Ability to work directly with clients to translate operational needs into technical solutions

 - Prior remote work experience is required. Candidates must be fluent in remote collaboration tools such as Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana, or similar platforms, and should ideally have worked with US or UK-based companies. Applications without this experience will not be considered.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with SEO tools such as Semrush, Ahrefs, BrightEdge, or Conductor

 - Familiarity with AI content platforms, LLM tooling, or marketing AI solutions

 - Startup or scale-up experience working across technical and operational roles




TOOLS & TECHNOLOGY

 - Python

 - SQL

 - SEO and marketing automation platforms

 - AI and LLM-based tools

 - Remote collaboration tools (Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana)

source_jobs.title: Marketing Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

Job Title Marketing Operations Manager

Location Remote

Time Zone US Business Hours (EST/CST)

Role Overview We are seeking a strategic and operationally driven Marketing Operations Manager to serve as the critical bridge between our marketing team and our manufacturing departments — including print, assembly, and CNC. This is a full-time role requiring someone who deeply understands how marketing campaigns translate into physical production, and who can keep people, processes, and timelines in alignment across a distributed, fast-moving team.

Key Responsibilities

 - Oversee marketing operations and ensure seamless alignment between the marketing team and manufacturing departments (print, assembly, and CNC)

 - Manage and supervise team members through daily huddles, one-on-ones, and structured check-ins

 - Streamline workflows and operational processes across marketing and production to maximise efficiency

 - Coordinate with department directors and cross-functional remote teams across multiple time zones

 - Track project timelines, deliverables, and KPIs to ensure consistent operational output and accountability

Required Qualifications

 - Proven experience in marketing operations, project management, or a related supervisory capacity

 - Solid understanding of marketing principles, campaign workflows, and how marketing strategy drives physical production

 - Demonstrated ability to manage and lead remote teams across different time zones

 - Strong organisational skills with the ability to juggle multiple projects and competing priorities simultaneously

 - Ability to work full US business hours (EST/CST)

 - Fluent in English — both written and spoken — with a clear, professional communication style

 - Must have prior remote work experience, be proficient with remote collaboration tools (such as Zoom, Asana, Monday.com http://Monday.com, Slack, or similar), and have ideally worked with US-based companies. Applications without this experience will not be considered.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience in sign-making, print, or physical product manufacturing environments

 - Prior experience working remotely with US-based companies

 - Familiarity with project management or collaboration tools such as Zoom, Asana, or Monday.com http://Monday.com

Tools & Technology

source_jobs.title: Social Content Strategist & Creator
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

Job Title Social Content Strategist & Creator – D2C Baby Subscription Brand

Location Remote

Time Zone Flexible – Deliverable-Based

Role Overview A growing D2C baby subscription brand is looking for a Social Content Strategist & Creator to lead its Instagram presence and replace a US agency with someone who truly gets the brand. This role is for a self-directed professional who can develop and execute a full social strategy, produce high-quality content that speaks to modern parents, and build a steady stream of influencer and UGC-driven material — all with minimal hand-holding. If you love building brands that feel real, relatable, and trustworthy, this is for you.

Key Responsibilities

Strategy & Planning

 - Develop and own the Instagram content strategy aligned with brand values and subscription growth goals

 - Build and manage a detailed content calendar that keeps the brand posting consistently and on-brand

 - Identify content gaps and opportunities, and proactively bring ideas to the table

Content Creation & Production

 - Create engaging short-form video and static content — authentic, human, and never AI-looking

 - Write captions that connect with moms and parents without sounding salesy

 - Select cover images, manage thumbnails, and ensure every post looks intentional and polished

UGC & Influencer Sourcing

 - Actively find and reach out to influencers and UGC creators in the parenting and lifestyle space

 - Build a content library and flywheel so fresh, authentic material is always available to publish

 - Coordinate approvals, repurposing rights, and cross-channel use of creator content

Performance & Iteration

 - Monitor Instagram analytics and adjust strategy based on engagement and growth trends

 - Report on what is working and evolve the approach with each content cycle

Required Qualifications

 - Native or near-native English fluency in both writing and speaking

 - A compelling portfolio showing real brand ownership — not just posts you were given to schedule

 - Demonstrably strong visual sensibility and content production skills beyond basic Canva

 - Hands-on experience running influencer or UGC coordination pipelines

 - Proven track record owning a social media presence from strategy through execution

 - Must have prior remote work experience with US or UK-based companies and proficiency with remote tools such as Asana, ClickUp, WhatsApp, or Google Workspace. Applications without this experience will not be considered.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with baby, parenting, family, or lifestyle consumer brands

 - Background working with D2C or subscription-based e-commerce businesses

 - Understanding of the US parenting demographic — what resonates, what turns them off

 - Willingness and comfort with on-camera appearances for content or candidate videos

Tools & Technology

 - Instagram and short-form video platforms

 - Advanced content creation and video editing tools (list your stack in your application)

 - Asana, ClickUp, or equivalent project management tools

 - Google Workspace, WhatsApp, and async communication platforms

Please NOTE It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps — the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Talent Systems & Recruiting Ops Builder
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.


JOB TITLE

Talent Systems & Recruiting Ops Builder




LOCATION

Remote




TIME ZONE

US time zones (EST–PST)




ROLE OVERVIEW

This role is for someone who loves turning chaos into clean, automated recruiting systems. You’ll build the “operating system” behind hiring—automating sourcing, referrals, market signals, interview readiness, and data quality so recruiters and hiring managers can move fast without breaking candidate experience. You’ll collaborate closely with talent, operators, and leaders—and ship improvements weekly.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own and evolve the recruiting tech + automation stack to support high-volume, high-quality hiring

 - Build scalable sourcing automation that delivers a steady flow of qualified, interested candidates per role

 - Launch competitor/talent signal monitoring with Slack alerts for key market changes (hiring spikes, layoffs, emerging talent)

 - Create a referral + backchannel engine that maps internal networks and highlights warm paths to candidates

 - Automate interviewer and candidate enablement (prep packets, interview guide library, triggered messaging, calendar workflows)

 - Improve the careers site to better “sell the story,” increase inbound quality, and reduce drop-off in the application flow

 - Put guardrails around ATS data (validation, exception reporting, cleanup routines) so reporting and coordination stay trustworthy
   
   


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - You can clearly map out (whiteboard-style) how you’d build the core systems listed above and what you’d ship first

 - 1+ year as a dedicated sourcer or recruiter, with strong instincts for what creates real pipeline (not vanity metrics)

 - Hands-on recruiting operations experience: process design, tooling, reporting, and stakeholder enablement

 - Practical builder mindset: you move from idea → prototype → working workflow fast, and keep improving it

 - Candidate-first approach: you optimize for clarity, speed, and a great experience for both candidates and interviewers

 - Non-negotiable: Prior remote work experience; fluent with remote collaboration tools (Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana or similar); and ideally have worked with US or UK-based companies—applications without this will not be considered
   
   


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Familiarity with modern recruiting automation tools (Clay, automation platforms, AI-assisted coding tools)

 - Ability to integrate systems using APIs/webhooks and maintain reliable workflows over time

 - Experience creating dashboards and quality loops (response rates, conversion, funnel health, data integrity)

 - Comfort partnering with brand/marketing to improve careers site content, SEO/AEO, and candidate conversion
   
   


TOOLS & TECHNOLOGY

Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana (or similar), ATS (Greenhouse/Lever or similar), Clay, Zapier/Make, Airtable/Notion, dashboards (Sheets/BI), APIs/webhooks, AI build tools (Cursor/Claude/v0)






PLEASE NOTE

It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps—the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Junior Graphic Designer & Marketing Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

 

Job Title
Junior Graphic Designer & Marketing Assistant

Location
Remote

Time Zone
US Time Zones (EST–PST)



ROLE OVERVIEW

We’re looking for a Junior Graphic Designer & Marketing Assistant to join a growing photography-focused startup. This is a junior, hands-on role ideal for someone early in their career who enjoys wearing multiple hats and wants to grow alongside a creative business.

Your primary focus will be graphic design, supporting visual content across marketing, social media, and client-facing materials. In addition, you’ll assist with influencer outreach, light administrative tasks, and occasional support with sales or partnership-related activities. This role is perfect for a highly organized, proactive individual with a startup mindset who enjoys creative work, learning new skills, and contributing wherever needed.

This is a fun, fast-paced environment centered around photography, creativity, and building a brand - so curiosity, energy, and flexibility are key.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create engaging graphic designs for social media, marketing campaigns, presentations, and digital assets

 - Support visual content needs for photography-related projects and promotions

 - Assist with influencer outreach, including researching creators, sending outreach messages, and tracking responses

 - Maintain basic outreach and contact lists, follow-ups, and status updates

 - Handle ad hoc virtual assistant and administrative tasks as needed (e.g., scheduling, organization, simple research)

 - Support basic sales or partnership activities, such as preparing materials or assisting with follow-ups

 - Help keep digital files, assets, and workflows organized

 - Collaborate with the founder and team to support day-to-day business needs

 - Bring creative ideas and contribute to a positive, startup-style working environment


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience (or strong practical experience) in graphic design or visual content creation

 - Proficiency with basic design tools such as Canva, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, or similar

 - Strong organizational and administrative skills, with attention to detail

 - Comfortable handling multiple small tasks in a fast-moving startup environment

 - Clear written communication skills and confidence reaching out to influencers or partners

 - Prior remote work experience is required, with fluency in remote collaboration tools such as Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana, or similar; candidates must have experience working with US- or UK-based companies - applications without this experience will not be considered


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Interest or background in photography, creative industries, or social media

 - Exposure to influencer marketing, outreach, or basic sales support

 - Familiarity with social media platforms (Instagram, TikTok, etc.)

 - Startup or small business experience

 - Willingness to learn new tools, platforms, and processes

source_jobs.title: Website Maintenance Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

Job Title Website Maintenance Specialist

Location Remote

Time Zone US Time Zones (EST–CST overlap required)

Role Overview We are seeking a detail-focused Website Maintenance Specialist to manage ongoing updates and quality assurance across a large portfolio of WordPress sites. This is a full-time role built for someone who takes pride in clean execution, thorough testing, and clear communication. You will work inside a structured ticketing system, handling everything from content edits to minor technical builds while maintaining a consistently high bar for quality.

Key Responsibilities

 - Process a daily queue of client website requests through a ticketing system, including content updates, design adjustments, and new landing pages

 - Execute WordPress changes spanning custom CSS/HTML, template edits, and basic-to-intermediate PHP and JavaScript

 - Conduct rigorous self-QA on every task: verify across browsers and devices, test all forms, links, menus, and interactive elements

 - Write professional client-facing update summaries for internal review before delivery

 - Work closely with developers and account managers to align on scope, requirements, and priorities

 - Identify patterns in recurring issues and surface recommendations for process or template improvements

 - Keep all sites secure, organized, and adhering to development best practices

Required Qualifications

 - Solid WordPress development background with hands-on experience in themes, plugins, child themes, and custom post types

 - Proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and working knowledge of PHP and JavaScript

 - Experience with leading page builders (Elementor, WPBakery, Divi, Gutenberg) and the ability to customize beyond their defaults

 - Track record of managing multiple live client sites simultaneously

 - Meticulous attention to detail and a self-QA mindset before every handoff

 - Professional written and spoken English with the ability to communicate clearly to non-technical stakeholders

 - Must have prior remote work experience, be fluent with remote collaboration tools (Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana, or similar), and have ideally worked with US or UK-based companies. Applications without this experience will not be considered.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Agency or multi-client environment experience

 - SEO knowledge applied within WordPress

 - Experience with site speed and performance optimization

 - Comfort using Git for version control

Tools & Technology

 - WordPress (themes, child themes, plugins, custom post types)

 - HTML5, CSS3, PHP, JavaScript

 - Page builders: Elementor, WPBakery, Divi, Gutenberg

 - Google Workspace

 - Slack, Zoom, and other remote collaboration tools

 - Ticketing / project management systems

Please NOTE It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps—the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

source_jobs.title: Revenue & Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

Job Title Revenue & Operations Manager

Location Remote

Time Zone Core Hours: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM Central Time (CT)

Role Overview We are hiring a Director of Growth & Operations to lead a pivotal transition from a project-based firm to a scalable, MRR-driven professional services business. This is a senior, hands-on leadership role where you will own both the revenue growth strategy and the operational infrastructure to deliver it—reporting directly to the Owner. If you have built a recurring-revenue services model before and thrive in lean, high-ownership environments, this role was designed for you.

Key Responsibilities

 - Own the full revenue engine: pipeline generation, sales execution, client onboarding, delivery quality, and retention

 - Lead a distributed global team across multiple time zones, managing performance through clear KPIs and weekly accountability rhythms

 - Drive MRR growth by coordinating with in-house and outsourced sales resources, and closing new business personally as needed

 - Define and enforce the MRR-first commercial model: packaging, pricing, scope controls, and engagement minimums

 - Run weekly sales and operations cadences; maintain live scorecards; conduct monthly reviews with the Owner

 - Improve client retention to 95%+ by strengthening onboarding processes, communication standards, and escalation protocols

 - Plan and manage team capacity so delivery quality is never sacrificed during growth phases

 - Maintain 13-week rolling cash flow visibility and surface financial risks before they become problems

 - Build the operational infrastructure—SOPs, templates, checklists—that makes performance consistent and scalable

 - Report and improve on core metrics weekly and monthly: MRR health, close rate, delivery timeliness, rework rate, and labor efficiency

Required Qualifications

 - Proven builder: you have grown a services business from early stage to $1–$5M in revenue and know what it takes to make that transition

 - You have owned both sides of the business: commercial growth (sales, pricing, pipeline) and operational execution (delivery, staffing, retention)

 - Direct experience selling and managing monthly recurring services: bookkeeping, accounting/CAS, fractional CFO, IT managed services, or closely related

 - Track record of improving retention through better onboarding, clearer scoping, and smarter escalation handling

 - Experience leading distributed or global teams, setting weekly priorities, and holding people accountable to outcomes

 - Scorecard-driven: you live and breathe KPIs and lead with data, not anecdote

 - Must have prior remote work experience, be fluent with remote collaboration platforms (Slack, Zoom, ClickUp, Google Workspace, or similar), and have ideally worked with US or UK-based companies. Applications without this experience will not be considered.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience targeting and closing $2M–$4M B2B service companies as clients

 - History of building and managing referral or partner ecosystems (accountants, attorneys, lenders, financial advisors)

 - High comfort operating without a big company infrastructure—you prefer ownership, speed, and scrappiness over bureaucracy

Tools & Technology

 - Slack

 - ClickUp

 - Google Workspace

 - Google Meet / Zoom

 - QuickBooks Online (QBO)

 - Canopy

 - UltraTax

Please NOTE It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps—the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

 
 
 
 
 

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: BPO Sales Development Representative

Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

source_jobs.title: Sales Lead
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

Job Title Sales Lead

Location Remote

Time Zone Core Hours: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM Central Time (CT)

Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Sales Lead to own and build the end-to-end revenue engine for a growing SMB-focused accounting firm. This is a 0-to-1 role — you're not supporting sales, you are the growth. You'll close long-term clients, improve the sales process, implement automation and AI, and help transition the business from a project-based model to a predictable monthly recurring revenue (MRR) model. If you've scaled a services business before and thrive in a scrappy, high-ownership environment, this role is for you.

Key Responsibilities

 - Own the full sales cycle — pipeline, closing, onboarding handoff, and retention/expansion

 - Build and optimize an MRR-first model with tight packaging, pricing, and scope boundaries

 - Personally implement automation and AI tools to increase monthly closes and sales efficiency

 - Lead sales meetings, ops meetings, scorecard reviews, and monthly KPI reviews with the Owner

 - Manage and develop direct reports — Lead Qualifier and Operations Analyst

 - Build and maintain SOPs, templates, and checklists to ensure repeatable, scalable results

 - Track and report KPIs including MRR mix, client retention, close rate, and pipeline coverage

 - Develop referral and partner channels (accounting firms, CFOs, attorneys, lenders, brokers, etc.)

 - Maintain a strong Google Maps presence and client-facing visibility

 - Handle sensitive client and company information with strict confidentiality

Required Qualifications

 - Proven 0→1 sales track record — you have scaled a services business from early stage to $1M–$5M in revenue

 - Experience selling monthly recurring services such as bookkeeping, accounting, fractional CFO, IT managed services, or similar

 - Demonstrated team leadership experience managing teams of 3–10 people

 - Strong scorecard discipline — you manage performance through KPIs and numbers

 - Experience managing distributed or global teams across time zones

 - Comfortable in a lean, fast-moving environment with minimal management layers

 - Experience working with US-based clients

Preferred Qualifications

 - Background in building or managing referral and partner channels

 - Prior experience in accounting, finance, consulting, or B2B professional services sales

 - Familiarity with transitioning businesses from one-off project models to recurring revenue

Tools & Technology

 - ClickUp, Canopy, Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Meet, Drive)

 - QuickBooks Online (QBO), Xero, Digits

 - UltraTax / Pro Connect

 - AI and automation tools for sales workflows

 - CRM and sales engagement platforms

Please NOTE It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps—the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

SENIOR ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE

🕐 Eastern Time Hours | 💰 Competitive Compensation | 🚀 High-Growth, Venture-Backed SaaS Startup


OVERVIEW

An emerging NYC-based SaaS company—backed by leading venture investors—is scaling rapidly and building next-generation solutions for enterprise marketers. Their platform helps brands optimize over $1M+ in annual ad spend, and they are assembling a top-performing founding GTM team to fuel the next phase of growth.

This opportunity is ideal for a senior-level sales professional with a proven record of closing complex enterprise deals. Candidates should be energized by fast-paced environments and comfortable running full-cycle sales motions at early-stage startups.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage the full sales cycle from outbound prospecting through to signed contract.

 - Sell multi-seat, multi-year SaaS deals to enterprise organizations, often exceeding six figures in annual contract value.

 - Leverage Gong.io http://Gong.io to analyze calls, refine messaging, and support team-level learning.

CANDIDATE PROFILE

Required Experience:

 - Experience selling into large brands, ideally within the advertising, marketing, or media space.

 - Fluency with modern sales tools including Salesforce, HubSpot, and Gong.io http://Gong.io.

 - Availability to work startup hours within the Eastern Time Zone.

Preferred Background:

 - History of closing deals exceeding $100K+ ARR.


WHY THIS ROLE

source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.

Job Title: Help Desk Support Technician
Location: Remote
Time Zone: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST


Role Overview
We are looking for an enthusiastic and eager-to-learn Entry-Level Help Desk Technician to join our fast-paced Managed Services Provider (MSP) environment. In this role you will serve as the first point of contact for inbound technical support requests, working alongside experienced engineers to deliver timely, accurate resolutions. If you are passionate about technology, eager to build your IT career, and ready to learn in a ticket-driven, SLA-focused setting, we want to hear from you.


What You'll Do

 - Receive, triage, and respond to inbound support tickets via phone, email, and ticketing platform within defined SLA windows.

 - Assist in diagnosing and troubleshooting basic hardware, software, networking, and connectivity issues for end users.

 - Support remote desktop sessions, basic account management tasks (Active Directory / Azure AD), and simple network troubleshooting under guidance.

 - Document all interactions, steps taken, and resolutions thoroughly in the PSA/ticketing system (e.g., ConnectWise, Autotask, HaloPSA).

 - Escalate complex or unresolved issues to senior engineers with clear, detailed handoff notes.

 - Assist with user onboarding and offboarding tasks including device setup and access provisioning.

 - Contribute to the internal knowledge base by documenting common fixes and workarounds.

 - Maintain professionalism and clear communication throughout the support lifecycle.


Required Qualifications

 - A genuine passion for IT and a strong desire to grow within a Managed Services Provider (MSP) environment.

 - Basic working knowledge of Windows 10/11, Microsoft 365, and common business applications.

 - Foundational understanding of networking concepts: TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, and VPN.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills with a customer-first mindset.

 - Ability to follow processes, take direction, and escalate appropriately.

 - Availability Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST.


Nice to Have

 - CompTIA A+ certification or currently working toward it.

 - Any exposure to PSA tools (ConnectWise, Autotask, or equivalent) or RMM platforms.

 - Basic familiarity with Azure Active Directory or Microsoft Intune.

 - Any hands-on experience in a helpdesk, IT internship, or lab environment.

source_jobs.description:

Job Title: Founding Performance Engineer
Location | On-Site — San Francisco, CA
Time Zone | PST


Role Overview:
We are seeking a Founding Performance Engineer to help build the technical backbone of a fast-moving AI video infrastructure startup. The company's first product turns prompts and documents into whiteboard-style explainer videos in seconds, with a bigger goal of making video generation as fast, cheap, and programmable as text generation is today. As one of the first engineering hires, you'll own the systems that determine how fast and efficient the product feels — going deep on real bottlenecks rather than surface-level fixes. This role suits someone who is energized by understanding exactly how software behaves under load, and who enjoys turning messy internals into clean, fast, dependable systems.



Key Responsibilities:
- Diagnose and resolve performance bottlenecks across code, media pipelines, and infrastructure
- Build tools for benchmarking, regression tracking, and performance testing
- Optimize concurrency, caching, batching, and scheduling across core workflows
- Improve GPU and compute resource utilization
- Design clean internal interfaces around complex, performance-critical systems
- Partner with the product team to keep improvements invisible but impactful to users



Required Qualifications:
- Strong grasp of systems fundamentals: latency, throughput, memory, CPU/GPU, and I/O
- Experience improving performance in real production software
- Proficiency in C++ or a similar low-level language, alongside backend development skills
- Ability to measure rigorously before optimizing, and explain tradeoffs clearly
- Comfortable using AI tools to increase speed, while relying on personal judgment to define and solve problems
- Must be willing to work on-site in San Francisco, CA



Preferred Qualifications:
- Background in robotics, embedded systems, or electronics engineering
- Comfortable working deep in the stack, below user-facing features
- Demonstrated history of measurable performance wins in production systems



Tools & Technology:
- C++ or similar low-level languages
-Backend/API frameworks and databases
- Cloud infrastructure and deployment tooling
- AI-assisted development tools

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Senior New Patient Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.


JOB TITLE

Senior New Patient Coordinator


LOCATION

Remote


TIME ZONE

US Time Zones (EST–PST) – Primary coverage aligned with East Coast hours




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a full-time Senior New Patient Coordinator to manage inbound patient inquiries—primarily via SMS—and conduct scheduled phone consultations as needed. This is not a high-volume, script-driven call center role. Instead, you will build trust through thoughtful, consultative conversations, guiding prospective patients toward the appropriate next step in their care journey.

This role requires consistent daytime availability and rapid response times when new inquiries are received. While lead flow may vary throughout the day, you are expected to remain attentive and ready to engage promptly. This position is ideal for someone empathetic, highly responsive, detail-oriented, and comfortable discussing health and wellness topics in a professional, patient-centered manner.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Monitor and respond promptly to inbound SMS inquiries with professionalism and warmth

 - Conduct consultative conversations to understand patient goals, treatment history, and concerns

 - Schedule consultations using Calendly or similar scheduling tools

 - Accurately document patient information and interactions within CRM and EHR systems

 - Execute timely, personalized follow-ups to support appointment conversion

 - Maintain strict patient confidentiality and ensure accurate record-keeping

 - Collaborate with internal team members to ensure smooth patient onboarding




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience in patient coordination, intake, client services, or a similar consultative role

 - Excellent written communication skills for SMS-based outreach and strong verbal communication for phone consultations

 - Proven ability to build rapport, demonstrate empathy, and guide conversations with confidence

 - Proficiency with CRM systems, scheduling tools, and managing multiple systems simultaneously

 - Basic to intermediate Excel skills (data entry, formatting, simple formulas)

 - Reliable high-speed internet and a professional remote work environment

 - Must have prior remote work experience, be fluent with remote collaboration tools and platforms (such as Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, Asana, or similar), and have ideally worked with US or UK-based companies. Applications without this experience will not be considered.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in healthcare, telehealth, wellness, chiropractic, physical therapy, concierge medicine, or related environments

 - Familiarity with Go High Level, DrChrono, or similar CRM/EHR systems

 - Experience handling SMS-based patient conversion or intake conversations

 - Background in healthcare administration or patient onboarding




TOOLS & TECHNOLOGY

 - Go High Level (CRM)

 - DrChrono (EHR)

 - Calendly or similar scheduling tools

 - SMS communication platforms

 - Google Workspace

 - Excel



Please NOTE

It is crucial that you complete the application form in full. As part of the application process, you will be required to record a video. If your application is successful, you will receive an email confirming next steps—the video is the first step of the interview process. If you do not record a video, we will not be able to consider you for ANY open roles.

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term career.


🚀 SENIOR ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE

🕐 Eastern Time Zone hours | 💰 Competitive compensation | 🔋 Venture-backed, high-growth tech firm



🏢 Company Overview

A New York–based startup—supported by top-tier venture investors—builds enterprise software that empowers global brands to monitor and optimize advertising budgets of $1 million+ annually. Rapid expansion has prompted the formation of a first-generation go-to-market team tasked with accelerating revenue growth.



🎯 Role Scope

Reporting to the CEO, the Senior Account Executive owns the complete sales life cycle—from pipeline creation to signed contract—within Fortune 1000–caliber accounts. Success demands expertise in navigating complex, multi-stakeholder environments and agility in the ever-shifting landscape of an early-stage company.



🔑 Key Responsibilities

 - Pipeline Leadership — Identify, qualify, and progress opportunities among enterprise prospects.

 - Consultative Discovery — Conduct in-depth needs analysis, deliver tailored demos, and craft ROI-focused business cases.

 - Deal Structuring — Negotiate and close multi-year, multi-seat contracts in partnership with legal and procurement teams.

 - Forecast Integrity — Maintain precise pipeline data in Salesforce and HubSpot, reporting weekly to executive leadership.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration — Partner with marketing, product, and customer success to refine messaging and influence the roadmap.

 - Performance Analysis — Leverage Gong.io http://Gong.io insights to fine-tune sales tactics and share best practices across the team.

 - Process Development — Contribute to playbooks, collateral, and market positioning as an early commercial hire.



✅ Required Qualifications

 - 6+ years of quota-carrying enterprise SaaS sales within U.S. venture-funded, high-growth firms.

 - Proven history closing complex, multi-stakeholder deals of $100K ARR+.

 - End-to-end sales mastery: prospecting, negotiation, executive engagement.

 - Power-user proficiency with Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, and related enablement tools.

 - Domain expertise in ad tech, performance analytics, or data platforms for media investment.

 - Outstanding written and verbal communication skills for both technical and business audiences.

 - Willingness to work core hours aligned with Eastern Time.



💡 Preferred Experience

 - Prior responsibility for building or formalizing a sales playbook in an early-stage setting.

 - Demonstrated success selling to marketing, analytics, or data-science stakeholders.
